jobs.title: Machine Learning Solutions Architect
jobs.body:
Join
phData
, a dynamic and innovative leader in the modern data stack.We partner with major cloud data platforms like Snowflake, AWS, Azure, GCP, Fivetran, Pinecone, Glean, and dbt to deliver cutting-edge services and solutions.
We're committed to helping global enterprises overcome their toughest data challenges.
phData is a remote-first global company with employees based in the United States, Latin America, and India.We celebrate the culture of each of our team members and foster a community of technological curiosity, ownership, and trust.Even though we're growing extremely fast, we maintain a casual, exciting work environment.We hire top performers and allow you the autonomy to deliver results.
6x Snowflake Partner of the Year (2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025)
Fivetran
,
dbt
, Atlation, and
AWS
Partner of the Year
#1 Partner in Snowflake Advanced Certifications
600+ Expert Cloud Certifications (Sigma, AWS, Azure, Dataiku, etc)
Recognized as an award-winning workplace in the
US
,
India
, and
LATAM
Machine Learning Engineers are the Swiss army knives of machine learning.They’re ready for anything, and they bring all the tools to ensure that data science models see the light of day.They own the infrastructure and deployment plan—from making sure data science models can actually be built using customer data to deploying them into a production environment, and everything in between. They provide thought leadership by recommending the right technologies and solutions for a given use case, from the application layer to infrastructure. Machine Learning Engineers have the team leadership and coding skills (e.g.Python, Java, and Scala) to get their solutions into production — and to help ensure performance, security, scalability, and robust data integration.
As a Solutions Architect on our Machine Learning Engineering team, you are responsible for:
Designing and implementing data solutions best suited to deliver on our customer needs — from model inference, retraining, monitoring, and beyond — across an evolving technical stack.
Providing thought leadership by recommending the technologies and solution design for a given use case, from the application layer to infrastructure; and they have the team leadership and coding skills (e.g.Python, Java, and Scala) to build and operate in production; and to help ensure performance, security, scalability, and robust data integration.
What you’ll do in this role:
Design and create environments for data scientists to build models and manipulate data
Work within customer systems to extract data and place it within an analytical environment
Learn and understand customer technology environments and systems
Define the deployment approach and infrastructure for models and be responsible for ensuring that businesses can use the models we develop
Demonstrate the business value of data by working with data scientists to manipulate and transform data into actionable insights
Reveal the true value of data by working with data scientists to manipulate and transform data into appropriate formats in order to deploy actionable machine learning models
Partner with data scientists to ensure solution deployability—at scale, in harmony with existing business systems and pipelines, and such that the solution can be maintained throughout its life cycle
Create operational testing strategies, validate and test the model in QA, and implementation, testing, and deployment
Ensure the quality of the delivered product
This job might be for you if you bring...
At least 6 years experience as a Machine Learning Engineer, Software Engineer, or Data Engineer
4-year Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related field
Experience deploying machine learning models in a production setting
Expertise in Python, Scala, Java, or another modern programming language
The ability to build and operate robust data pipelines using a variety of data sources, programming languages, and toolsets
Strong working knowledge of SQL and the ability to write, debug, and optimize distributed SQL queries
Hands-on experience in one or more big data ecosystem products/languages such as Spark, Snowflake, Databricks, etc.
Familiarity with multiple data sources (e.g.JMS, Kafka, RDBMS, DWH, MySQL, Oracle, SAP)
Systems-level knowledge in network/cloud architecture, operating systems (e.g., Linux), and storage systems (e.g., AWS, Databricks, Cloudera)
Production experience in core data technologies (e.g.Spark, HDFS, Snowflake, Databricks, Redshift, & Amazon EMR)
Development of APIs and web server applications (e.g.Flask, Django, Spring)
Complete software development lifecycle experience, including design, documentation, implementation, testing, and deployment
Excellent communication and presentation skills; previous experience working with internal or external customers
You might also have...
A Master’s or other advanced degree in data science or a related field
Hands-on experience with one or more ecosystem technologies (e.g., Spark, Databricks, Snowflake, AWS/Azure/GCP)
Relevant side projects (e.g.contributions to an open source technology stack)
Experience working with Data-Science and Machine-Learning software and libraries such as h2o, TensorFlow, Keras, scikit-learn, etc.
Experience with Docker, Kubernetes, or some other containerization technology
AWS Sagemaker (or Azure ML) and MLflow experience
Experience building enterprise ML models
Why phData?We offer:
Remote-First Work Environment
Casual, award-winning small-business work environment
Collaborative culture that prizes autonomy, creativity, and transparency
Competitive comp, excellent benefits, generous weeks PTO plus 10 Holidays (and other cool perks)
Accelerated learning and professional development through advanced training and certifications
phData celebrates diversity and is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.Our approach helps us to build a winning team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities.So, regardless of how your diversity expresses itself, you can find a home here at phData.We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer.We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, veteran status, genetic information, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.If you would like to request an accommodation due to a disability, please contact us at People Operations.

Experience with Docker, Kubernetes, or some other containerization technology
AWS Sagemaker (or Azure ML) and MLflow experience
Experience building enterprise ML models

Collaborative culture that prizes autonomy, creativity, and transparency

jobs.file: phdjd001 - Machine Learning - Senior Machine Learning Engineer.txt
jobs.title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer
jobs.body:
Join
phData
, a dynamic and innovative leader in the modern data stack.We partner with major cloud data platforms like Snowflake, AWS, Azure, GCP, Fivetran, Pinecone, Glean, and dbt to deliver cutting-edge services and solutions.
We're committed to helping global enterprises overcome their toughest data challenges.
phData is a remote-first global company with employees based in the United States, Latin America, and India.We celebrate the culture of each of our team members and foster a community of technological curiosity, ownership, and trust.Even though we're growing extremely fast, we maintain a casual, exciting work environment.We hire top performers and allow you the autonomy to deliver results.
6x Snowflake Partner of the Year (2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025)
Fivetran
,
dbt
, Atlation, and
AWS
Partner of the Year
#1 Partner in Snowflake Advanced Certifications
600+ Expert Cloud Certifications (Sigma, AWS, Azure, Dataiku, etc)
Recognized as an award-winning workplace in the
US
,
India
, and
LATAM
Machine Learning Engineers are the Swiss army knives of machine learning.They’re ready for anything, and they bring all the tools to ensure that data science models see the light of day.They own the infrastructure and deployment plan—from making sure data science models can actually be built using customer data to deploying them into a production environment, and everything in between. They provide thought leadership by recommending the right technologies and solutions for a given use case, from the application layer to infrastructure. Machine Learning Engineers have the team leadership and coding skills (e.g.Python, Java, and Scala) to get their solutions into production — and to help ensure performance, security, scalability, and robust data integration.
What you’ll do in this role:
Design and create environments for data scientists to build models and manipulate data
Work within customer systems to extract data and place it within an analytical environment
Learn and understand customer technology environments and systems
Define the deployment approach and infrastructure for models and be responsible for ensuring that businesses can use the models we develop
Reveal the true value of data by working with data scientists to manipulate and transform data into appropriate formats in order to deploy actionable machine learning models
Partner with data scientists to ensure solution deployability—at scale, in harmony with existing business systems and pipelines, and such that the solution can be maintained throughout its life cycle
Create operational testing strategies, validate and test the model in QA, and implementation, testing, and deployment
Ensure the quality of the delivered product
Required Experience:
At least 4 years experience as a Machine Learning Engineer, Software Engineer, or Data Engineer
4-year Bachelor's degree in Computer Engineering or a related field
Experience deploying data science models in a production setting.
Expertise in Python, Scala, Java, or another modern programming language
The ability to build and operate robust data pipelines using a variety of data sources, programming languages, and toolsets
Strong working knowledge of SQL and the ability to write, debug, and optimize distributed SQL queries
Experience working with Data Science/Machine Learning software and libraries such as h2o, TensorFlow, Keras, scikit-learn, etc.
Experience with Docker, Kubernetes, or some other containerization technology
Familiarity with multiple data source systems (e.g.JMS, Kafka, RDBMS, DWH, MySQL, Oracle, SAP)
Systems-level knowledge in network/cloud architecture, operating systems (e.g., Linux), storage systems (e.g., AWS, Databricks, Cloudera)
Production experience in core data technologies (e.g.Spark, Pandas)
Development of APIs and web server applications (e.g.Flask, Django, Spring)
Complete software development lifecycle experience including design, documentation, ong analytical abilities; ability to translate business requirements and use cases into a solution, including ingestion of many data sources, ETL processing, data access, and consumption, as well as custom analytics
Excellent communication and presentation skills; previous experience working with internal or external customers
Preferred Experience
A Master’s or other advanced degree in data science or a related field
Hands-on experience with one or more ecosystem technologies (e.g., HBase, Impala, Solr, Kudu, Streamsets, NiFi, ElasticSearch, Databricks, Snowflake, AWS/Azure/GCP)
Relevant side projects (e.g.contributions to an open source technology stack)
AWS Sagemaker, MLFlow experience
Why phData?We offer:
Remote-First Work Environment
Casual, award-winning small-business work environment
Collaborative culture that prizes autonomy, creativity, and transparency
Competitive comp, excellent benefits, generous PTO plus 10 Holidays (and other cool perks)
Accelerated learning and professional development through advanced training and certifications
phData celebrates diversity and is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.Our approach helps us to build a winning team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities.So, regardless of how your diversity expresses itself, you can find a home here at phData.We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer.We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, veteran status, genetic information, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.If you would like to request an accommodation due to a disability, please contact us at People Operations.

jobs.file: phdjd001 - Managed Services - Principal Solution Architect.txt
jobs.title: Principal Solution Architect
jobs.body:
Join
phData
, a dynamic and innovative leader in the modern data stack.We partner with major cloud data platforms like Snowflake, AWS, Azure, GCP, Fivetran, Pinecone, Glean, and dbt to deliver cutting-edge services and solutions.
We're committed to helping global enterprises overcome their toughest data challenges.
phData is a remote-first global company with employees based in the United States, Latin America, and India.We celebrate the culture of each of our team members and foster a community of technological curiosity, ownership, and trust.Even though we're growing extremely fast, we maintain a casual, exciting work environment.We hire top performers and allow you the autonomy to deliver results.
6x Snowflake Partner of the Year (2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025)
Fivetran
,
dbt
, Atlation, and
AWS
Partner of the Year
#1 Partner in Snowflake Advanced Certifications
600+ Expert Cloud Certifications (Sigma, AWS, Azure, Dataiku, etc)
Recognized as an award-winning workplace in the
US
,
India
, and
LATAM
Role Overview
We are looking for a Principal Solutions Architect – Managed Services to join our Managed Services team.In this role, you will lead the architecture, operation, and continuous improvement of modern cloud-native data platforms for multiple customers, ensuring they are secure, scalable, and high-performing.You will collaborate closely with clients, engineers, and practice leadership to translate evolving requirements into actionable roadmaps and high-quality outcomes across streaming, data lakes, and analytics workloads.
Key Responsibilities
Client Delivery
Own and drive the design, provisioning, and enhancement of cloud-native data solutions on AWS and/or Azure, leveraging deep expertise in Snowflake and related data services for enterprise-scale platforms.
Lead the operation and management of modern data platforms—from streaming and data lakes to analytics—across a progressively evolving technical stack for multiple concurrent customer accounts.
Translate business and technical requirements into Epics, Stories, and Tasks that mature and improve customers’ data platforms, ensuring timely and successful delivery of these initiatives.
Provide architectural and operational guidance for cloud-native data warehouses (e.g., Snowflake, Redshift) and distributed storage technologies (e.g., S3, ADLS, NoSQL) to support large user bases.
Recommend appropriate technologies and approaches to ensure performance, security, scalability, and user satisfaction, including data model design for data warehouses and data lakes.
Continually identify and deliver automation and optimization opportunities that expand and harden customers’ data platforms and phData’s managed services offerings.
Collaboration & Leadership
Serve as the technical leader across multiple accounts, providing clear ownership, direction, and decision-making while navigating complex customer environments.
Delegate work effectively, coach engineers, and mentor team members, fostering upskilling and continuous learning across evolving technology stacks.
Collaborate across technology stacks and organizational silos to integrate disparate functions and technologies into cohesive solutions.
Create and deliver detailed technical presentations and recommendations for executive and senior stakeholders, articulating complex concepts clearly and confidently.
Demonstrate calm, structured thinking under pressure—quickly assessing situations, formulating plans of action, and following through to successful outcomes.
Manage the human side of delivery, engaging respectfully and confidently with teammates and customers, including during challenging conversations.
Practice & Firm Contribution
Create, challenge, and improve processes and procedures to enhance the quality, consistency, and scalability of managed services delivery.
Build automated solutions for repetitive administrative and operational tasks using Python or similar tooling to increase efficiency and reliability.
Contribute to internal knowledge sharing, training, and upskilling initiatives that raise the technical bar across the team.
Help refine and standardize patterns, runbooks, and best practices for modern data platforms, workflows, and CI/CD across the practice.
Additional Responsibilities
Act as a trusted advisor to senior client stakeholders, shaping roadmaps, influencing strategic decisions, and guiding long-term initiatives.
Mentor and coach team members, fostering a culture of learning, feedback, and continuous improvement.
Help define and refine practice standards, reusable assets, and delivery frameworks.
About You
You are a client-focused technical leader and problem solver who thrives in fast-paced, operations-heavy environments with multiple concurrent customers and evolving requirements.You enjoy tackling complex data challenges, working across technology stacks and organizational boundaries, and turning ambiguity into structured plans and real outcomes.You bring strong presence, clear communication, and a collaborative mindset, valuing authenticity, psychological safety, and follow-through with teammates and clients alike.
Required Qualifications
Experience
15+ years of experience architecting, operating, and optimizing modern cloud-based data platforms and data warehouses in enterprise environments.
Technical / Functional Skills
Advanced data platform expertise in Snowflake and at least one major cloud platform (AWS and/or Azure), with experience in Databricks or similar technologies.
Hands-on experience with cloud and distributed data storage technologies such as S3, ADLS, Cassandra, or other NoSQL systems.
Strong background in data integration technologies, including Spark, Kafka, event/streaming platforms, and tools such as Matillion, Fivetran, HVR, AWS Data Migration Services, and Azure Data Factory.
Production experience with workflow management and orchestration tools such as Airflow, AWS Managed Airflow, and Luigi.
Strong SQL skills, including the ability to write, debug, and optimize complex queries for performance and reliability.
Extensive experience with Infrastructure as Code using Terraform or CloudFormation.
Experience building automated solutions for repetitive administrative or operational tasks using Python or a similar language.
Familiarity with CI/CD and deployment frameworks, including tools like Bitbucket, GitHub, Flyway, and Liquibase.
Active daily use of AI coding tools (Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Claude, ChatGPT, or equivalent) with demonstrated judgment about when to trust, verify, and correct AI-generated output.
Ability to describe specific engineering tasks completed with AI assistance.
Consulting / Delivery Skills
Experience delivering projects and managed services for external or internal clients in a professional services, consulting, or complex enterprise environment.
Ability to break down complex, multifaceted data challenges into structured, actionable steps and drive them through to completion.
Proven experience as a technical team lead and mentor, providing direction, coaching, and quality oversight for engineers.
Strong client-facing written and verbal communication skills, including comfort presenting detailed technical content to executive audiences.
Collaboration & Ownership
Demonstrated ability to work effectively with distributed, cross-functional teams across platforms, applications, and organizational boundaries.
Professional track record of creating, challenging, and improving processes and procedures to enhance delivery quality and efficiency.
Unmatched troubleshooting and performance tuning skills, with a strong sense of ownership and follow-through.
Presence and poise to manage the human side of delivery, including difficult conversations, while upholding a respectful and collaborative culture.
Education
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
Preferred Qualifications
Preferred qualifications help candidates stand out but are not required for success in this role.
Snowflake SnowPro Core certification.
One or more Snowflake advanced certifications.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a related field.
Experience in managed services, operations-focused roles, or similar environments supporting multiple customers simultaneously.
Prior experience working in global or remote teams and partnering across US, LATAM, and/or India.
Contributions to open source, technical communities, speaking, writing, or internal accelerators are a plus.
Location & Time Zone Expectations
This role is based in US and operates primarily in Central and/or Eastern time zones with significant overlap to US Central Time.
We are a remote-first company, and you should be comfortable working with a distributed global team.
Some flexibility may be required to collaborate across time zones with colleagues and clients.
Client needs may occasionally require flexibility in working hours to support key milestones or workshops.
Why phData?
Impactful Work:
Partner with leading organizations on meaningful data & AI initiatives.
Collaborative Culture:
Work with a supportive, high-performing global team that values transparency, autonomy, and continuous improvement.
Growth Opportunities:
Access to challenging projects, mentorship, and structured development pathways.
Values-Driven:
We prioritize doing the right thing for our clients, our teams, and our community.
Benefits at phData
US:
Remote-First Work Environment
401k plan with company match
Medical, Dental and Vision insurance
Home Office Equipment Stipend
Annual stipend for Learning and Development
Competitive comp, excellent benefits, 4 weeks PTO plan plus 10 Holidays (and other cool perks)
phData celebrates diversity and is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.Our approach helps us to build a winning team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities.So, regardless of how your diversity expresses itself, you can find a home here at phData.We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer.We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, veteran status, genetic information, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.If you would like to request an accommodation due to a disability, please contact us at People Operations.

jobs.file: phdjd001 - Managed Services - Senior Solutions Architect (Managed Services).txt
jobs.title: Senior Solutions Architect (Managed Services)
jobs.body:
Join
phData
, a dynamic and innovative leader in the modern data stack.We partner with major cloud data platforms like Snowflake, AWS, Azure, GCP, Fivetran, Pinecone, Glean, and dbt to deliver cutting-edge services and solutions.
We're committed to helping global enterprises overcome their toughest data challenges.
phData is a remote-first global company with employees based in the United States, Latin America, and India.We celebrate the culture of each of our team members and foster a community of technological curiosity, ownership, and trust.Even though we're growing extremely fast, we maintain a casual, exciting work environment.We hire top performers and allow you the autonomy to deliver results.
6x Snowflake Partner of the Year (2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025)
Fivetran
,
dbt
, Atlation, and
AWS
Partner of the Year
#1 Partner in Snowflake Advanced Certifications
600+ Expert Cloud Certifications (Sigma, AWS, Azure, Dataiku, etc)
Recognized as an award-winning workplace in the
US
,
India
, and
LATAM
Role Overview
We are looking for a Sr.Solutions Architect – Managed Services to join our Elastic Platform Operations (EPO) team. In this role, you will operate, mature, and optimize modern cloud data platforms spanning streaming, data lakes, data warehouses, and analytics to ensure performance, security, scalability, and user satisfaction.You will collaborate closely with clients, internal engineering and support teams, and practice leadership to deliver high-quality solutions and advance phData’s delivery excellence.
Key Responsibilities
Client Delivery
Own and drive the operation, reliability, and continuous improvement of modern cloud data platforms (streaming, data lakes, data warehouses, and analytics) for enterprise customers.
Translate business and technical requirements into robust cloud-native data platform architectures and implementation plans that align with phData methodologies, standards, and best practices.
Ensure engagements are delivered on time, within scope, and with measurable business value by providing architectural guidance and hands-on operational support for platforms such as Snowflake and Amazon Redshift.
Proactively identify and articulate best practices, platform optimizations, and automation opportunities to help customers get the most value from their Cloud Data Platform.
Collaboration & Leadership
Collaborate with cross-functional partners (engineering, analytics, ML, support, and others) to design, build, and operate modern data platforms for our clients.
Act as a technical leader across multiple simultaneous customer accounts, skillfully navigating complex environments and aligning stakeholders on priorities and approaches.
Provide technical and strategic leadership during workshops, discovery sessions, design reviews, and operational reviews, including creating and delivering detailed technical presentations.
Ensure high quality in deliverables through rigorous troubleshooting, performance tuning, documentation, testing, governance, and change management.
Partner with practice and account leaders to build Epics, Stories, and Tasks that mature and improve customer data platforms, delegating effectively to Engineers and ensuring successful and timely delivery.
Practice & Firm Contribution
Contribute to internal initiatives such as codifying operational best practices, building accelerators, templates, and playbooks, and enhancing our cloud data platform offerings.
Continually hunt for ways to automate and optimize existing processes, tooling, and workflows, and share improvements with the broader team.
Train and mentor engineers on new technology stacks, operational practices, and platform standards.
Represent phData with professionalism in all interactions, communicating clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Additional Responsibilities
Act as a trusted advisor to senior client stakeholders, shaping data platform roadmaps, influencing strategic decisions, and guiding long-term initiatives.
Mentor and coach team members, fostering a culture of learning, feedback, and continuous improvement in cloud data platform operations.
Help define and refine practice standards, reusable assets, and delivery frameworks for cloud-native data platforms and managed services.
Lead complex incident response and post-incident review efforts, driving systemic improvements that enhance performance, reliability, and user satisfaction.
About You
You are a client-focused technical leader and problem solver who enjoys operating and improving complex cloud data platforms, driving automation, and coaching engineers to deliver reliable, scalable solutions.You are comfortable working in a fast-paced, outcomes-driven environment with distributed, global teams and partnering closely with clients across time zones.
Required Qualifications
Experience
10+ years of experience in modern cloud data platforms, data warehousing, consulting, and data engineering/operations.
Technical / Functional Skills
Extensive experience providing architectural guidance and operational support for cloud-native data warehouses such as Snowflake and/or Amazon Redshift across a large user base.
Expertise with cloud-native data technologies in AWS and/or Azure, including services such as S3, Aurora, Athena, Redshift, SageMaker, Azure SQL/DevOps, and Azure Data Factory.
Production support experience in large enterprise environments supporting cloud data platforms and a variety of cloud data technologies.
Strong working knowledge of SQL, with the ability to write, debug, and optimize complex queries for performance and maintainability.
Strong troubleshooting, performance tuning, and database administration skills for cloud data platforms.
Active daily use of AI coding tools (Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Claude, ChatGPT, or equivalent) with demonstrated judgment about when to trust, verify, and correct AI-generated output.
Ability to describe specific engineering tasks completed with AI assistance.
Consulting / Delivery Skills
Experience delivering projects for external or internal clients in a professional services or consulting environment.
Ability to break down complex, ambiguous platform challenges into structured, actionable steps and drive them through to completion.
Strong written and verbal communication skills in English, including the ability to create and deliver detailed presentations to client and internal audiences.
Collaboration & Ownership
Demonstrated ability to work effectively with distributed and cross-functional teams (including engineering, analytics, and support) to ensure seamless project delivery and high customer satisfaction.
Proven track record of taking ownership, managing multiple priorities across several customer accounts, and delivering high-quality work with minimal supervision.
Proven experience learning new technology stacks, training team members, and creating, challenging, and improving processes and procedures.
Education
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
Preferred Qualifications
Preferred qualifications help candidates stand out but are not required for success in this role.
Production and/or database administration experience and certifications in core data platforms such as Snowflake, AWS, Azure, GCP, Hadoop, or Databricks.
Production experience with cloud and distributed data storage technologies such as S3, ADLS, HDFS, GCS, Kudu, Elasticsearch/Solr, Cassandra, or other NoSQL storage systems.
Production experience with data integration technologies such as Spark, Kafka, event/streaming platforms, StreamSets, Matillion, Fivetran, HVR, NiFi, AWS Data Migration Service, Azure Data Factory, Google Dataproc, or similar tools.
Production experience with workflow management and orchestration tools such as Airflow, AWS Managed Airflow, Luigi, or NiFi.
Working experience with infrastructure as code using Terraform or CloudFormation.
Strong expertise in a scripting language (preferably Python) to automate repetitive tasks and improve operational efficiency.
Experience with continuous integration and deployment frameworks and tools such as Bitbucket, GitHub, Flyway, and Liquibase.
Experience as a technical team lead, providing direction, coaching, and support to engineers.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a related field.
Location & Time Zone Expectations
This role is based in the US and operates primarily in Central and Eastern time zones.
We are a remote-first company, and you should be comfortable working with a distributed global team.
Some flexibility may be required to collaborate across time zones with colleagues and clients.
Client needs may occasionally require flexibility in working hours to support key milestones or workshops.
Why phData?
Impactful Work:
Partner with leading organizations on meaningful data & AI initiatives.
Collaborative Culture:
Work with a supportive, high-performing global team that values transparency, autonomy, and continuous improvement.
Growth Opportunities:
Access to challenging projects, mentorship, and structured development pathways.
Values-Driven:
We prioritize doing the right thing for our clients, our teams, and our community.
Benefits at phData
US:
Remote-First Work Environment
401k plan with company match
Dental and Vision insurance
Home Office Equipment Stipend
Annual stipend for Learning and Development
Competitive comp, excellent benefits, 4 weeks PTO plan plus 10 Holidays (and other cool perks)
phData celebrates diversity and is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.Our approach helps us to build a winning team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities.So, regardless of how your diversity expresses itself, you can find a home here at phData.We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer.We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, veteran status, genetic information, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.If you would like to request an accommodation due to a disability, please contact us at People Operations.

jobs.file: phdjd001 - Managed Services - Solutions Architect (Managed Services).txt
jobs.title: Solutions Architect (Managed Services)
jobs.body:
Join
phData
, a dynamic and innovative leader in the modern data stack.We partner with major cloud data platforms like Snowflake, AWS, Azure, GCP, Fivetran, Pinecone, Glean, and dbt to deliver cutting-edge services and solutions.
We're committed to helping global enterprises overcome their toughest data challenges.
phData is a remote-first global company with employees based in the United States, Latin America, and India.We celebrate the culture of each of our team members and foster a community of technological curiosity, ownership, and trust.Even though we're growing extremely fast, we maintain a casual, exciting work environment.We hire top performers and allow you the autonomy to deliver results.
6x Snowflake Partner of the Year (2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025)
Fivetran
,
dbt
, Atlation, and
AWS
Partner of the Year
#1 Partner in Snowflake Advanced Certifications
600+ Expert Cloud Certifications (Sigma, AWS, Azure, Dataiku, etc)
Recognized as an award-winning workplace in the
US
,
India
, and
LATAM
Role Overview
We are looking for a Solutions Architect to join our Elastic Operations (Managed Services) team.In this role, you will lead the design, operation, and continuous improvement of large-scale cloud-native data platforms, ensuring reliability, performance, security, and scalability across Snowflake and leading cloud services on AWS and Azure.You will collaborate closely with clients, internal engineering teams, and practice leadership to translate complex business and operational requirements into actionable architectures and roadmaps, helping customers mature their data platforms and derive measurable value.You will also play a key role in standardizing patterns, driving automation, and elevating phData’s Elastic Operations delivery excellence.
Key Responsibilities
Client Delivery
Own and drive the architecture, implementation, and ongoing operation of modern cloud-native data platforms (Snowflake on AWS/Azure) for multiple enterprise customers across data lakes, streaming, and analytics use cases.
Translate business, security, and operational requirements into scalable, cost-effective platform designs leveraging services such as Snowflake, IAM, S3, EC2, Kinesis, SageMaker, Kafka, Azure Data Factory, ADLS, Fivetran, dbt, Airflow, and related technologies.
Ensure engagements are delivered on time, within scope, and with measurable business value for clients through proactive monitoring, incident management, performance tuning, capacity planning, and continuous optimization.
Collaboration & Leadership
Collaborate with cross-functional partners including data engineers, platform engineers, data scientists, architects, and project managers to deliver successful Elastic Operations engagements.
Lead and mentor technical teams operating and managing customer platforms, including building and managing Epics, Stories, and Tasks, delegating work, and ensuring high-quality, timely delivery across a progressively evolving technical stack.
Ensure high quality in deliverables through code and configuration reviews, documentation, runbooks, testing, governance practices, and change management that align with phData standards.
Partner with practice and account leaders to identify opportunities to expand engagements, mature client platforms, standardize patterns, and introduce automation to reduce toil and improve reliability.
Practice & Firm Contribution
Contribute to internal initiatives such as developing automation, accelerators, templates, and playbooks for Elastic Operations, including infrastructure-as-code, monitoring, and operational tooling.
Help refine operational processes, procedures, and best practices for running modern data platforms at scale, including incident response, problem management, and continuous improvement.
Support the growth and enablement of the team by sharing knowledge, contributing to internal training, and providing coaching and guidance to engineers on cloud, data, and operations topics.
Represent phData with professionalism in all interactions, communicating clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders and advocating for best practices in cloud data architecture and operations.
Additional Responsibilities
Act as a trusted advisor to senior client stakeholders, shaping platform roadmaps, influencing strategic decisions, and guiding long-term modernization and optimization initiatives.
Mentor and coach team members, fostering a culture of learning, feedback, operational excellence, and continuous improvement across the Elastic Operations practice.
Help define and refine practice standards, reusable assets, automation frameworks, and delivery methodologies for managed services and Elastic Operations at phData.
Lead high-impact technical and architectural discussions, including data model and platform design reviews, and drive alignment across customer and internal teams.
About You
You are a hands-on, client-focused technical leader who enjoys solving complex data and platform challenges in a fast-paced, outcomes-driven environment.You are comfortable navigating ambiguity, operating across multiple customers and technology stacks at once, and breaking big problems into actionable steps.You think holistically about technical, business, operational, and human dimensions, communicate clearly with executives and engineers alike, and take ownership to follow through on commitments.You value psychological safety, collaboration, and continuous learning, and you thrive in a global, distributed team.
Required Qualifications
Experience
10+ years of experience in designing, operating, and supporting cloud-native data platforms and enterprise data ecosystems, ideally across Snowflake and major cloud providers (AWS and/or Azure).
Technical / Functional Skills
Production support and architectural experience with cloud-native data warehouses (Snowflake and/or Redshift) and cloud platforms (AWS and/or Azure), including IAM, storage (S3/ADLS), and compute services.
Production experience with distributed and NoSQL storage technologies such as S3, ADLS, HDFS, Elasticsearch/Solr, Cassandra, or similar, and with modern data architectures (data lakes, streaming, analytics platforms).
Hands-on experience with data integration, streaming, and orchestration tools such as Spark, Kafka, Fivetran, HVR, NiFi, AWS Data Migration Services, Azure Data Factory, Airflow/Managed Airflow, Luigi, or similar technologies.
Strong working knowledge of SQL with the ability to write, debug, and optimize complex queries for performance, reliability, and cost.
Demonstrated ability to design, implement, and improve operational processes, procedures, and automation (e.g., monitoring, alerting, runbooks, performance tuning) for enterprise-scale data platforms.
Consulting / Delivery Skills
Experience delivering projects for external or internal clients in a professional services, managed services, or consulting environment.
Ability to break down complex problems into structured, actionable steps and drive them through to completion across multiple concurrent customer engagements.
Strong written and verbal communication skills in English.
Experience creating and delivering clear, executive-ready technical presentations and recommendations.
Collaboration & Ownership
Demonstrated ability to work effectively with distributed and cross-functional teams across engineering, operations, and business stakeholders.
Proven track record of taking ownership, managing multiple priorities and customer accounts, and delivering high-quality work with minimal supervision, including mentoring and guiding other engineers.
Education - If desired
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
Preferred Qualifications
Extensive experience with infrastructure as code using Terraform or AWS CloudFormation for provisioning and managing cloud data platform components.
Proven expertise using Python to build automated solutions for repetitive support and operational tasks.
Experience with continuous integration and deployment frameworks and tools such as Bitbucket, GitHub, Flyway, Liquibase, or similar.
Snowflake SnowPro Core certification.
Experience in a managed services or Elastic Operations model, supporting multiple customers and platforms simultaneously.
Prior experience working in global or remote teams and partnering across US, LATAM, and/or India.
Location & Time Zone Expectations
This role is based in India and operates primarily in India Standard Time (IST), with overlap as needed to support global clients and teams.
We are a remote-first company, and you should be comfortable working with a distributed global team.
Some flexibility may be required to collaborate across time zones with colleagues and clients.
Client needs may occasionally require flexibility in working hours to support key milestones or workshops.
Why phData?
Impactful Work:
Partner with leading organizations on meaningful data & AI initiatives.
Collaborative Culture:
Work with a supportive, high-performing global team that values transparency, autonomy, and continuous improvement.
Growth Opportunities:
Access to challenging projects, mentorship, and structured development pathways.
Values-Driven:
We prioritize doing the right thing for our clients, our teams, and our community.
Benefits at phData
Remote-First Workplace
Medical Insurance for Self & Family
Medical Insurance for Parents
Term Life & Personal Accident
Wellness Allowance
Broadband Reimbursement
Continuous learning and growth opportunities to enhance your skills and expertise
Other benefits include paid certifications, professional development allowance, and bonuses for creating for company-approved content

platforms). hands-on experience with data integration, streaming, and orchestration tools such as spark, kafka, fivetran, hvr, nifi, aws data migration services, azure data factory, airflow/managed clear, executive-ready technical presentations and recommendations. collaboration & ownership demonstrated ability to work effectively with distributed and cross-functional teams across engineering, operations, and business stakeholders. proven track record of taking ownership, managing multiple priorities and customer accounts, and delivering high-quality work with minimal supervision, including mentoring and guiding other engineers. education - if desired bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience. preferred qualifications extensive experience with infrastructure as code using terraform or aws cloudformation for provisioning and managing cloud data platform components. proven expertise using python to build automated solutions for repetitive support and operational tasks. experience with continuous integration and deployment frameworks and tools such as bitbucket, github, flyway, liquibase, or similar. snowflake snowpro core certification. experience in a managed services or elastic operations model, supporting multiple customers and platforms simultaneously. prior experience working in global or remote teams jobs.file: phdjd001 - Managed Services - Solutions Architect - LATAM - Remote.txt
jobs.title: Solutions Architect
jobs.body:
Join
phData
, a dynamic and innovative leader in the modern data stack.We partner with major cloud data platforms like Snowflake, AWS, Azure, GCP, Fivetran, Pinecone, Glean, and dbt to deliver cutting-edge services and solutions.
We're committed to helping global enterprises overcome their toughest data challenges.
phData is a remote-first global company with employees based in the United States, Latin America, and India.We celebrate the culture of each of our team members and foster a community of technological curiosity, ownership, and trust.Even though we're growing extremely fast, we maintain a casual, exciting work environment.We hire top performers and allow you the autonomy to deliver results.
6x Snowflake Partner of the Year (2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025)
Fivetran
,
dbt
, Atlation, and
AWS
Partner of the Year
#1 Partner in Snowflake Advanced Certifications
600+ Expert Cloud Certifications (Sigma, AWS, Azure, Dataiku, etc)
Recognized as an award-winning workplace in the
US
,
India
, and
LATAM
Role Overview
We are looking for a Solutions Architect – Managed Services to join our Elastic Platform Operations (EPO) team.In this role, you will lead the design, implementation, and ongoing optimization of cloud-native data platforms on Snowflake, AWS, and Azure for our managed services clients, ensuring they are secure, scalable, and aligned to strategic business goals.You will collaborate closely with clients, internal engineering and support teams, and practice leadership to deliver high-quality solutions, maximize platform ROI, and advance phData’s delivery excellence.
Key Responsibilities
Client Delivery
Own and drive end-to-end solution architecture, design, and implementation of large-scale, cloud-native data platform projects on Snowflake, AWS, and Azure for managed services customers.
Translate business and technical requirements into secure, scalable platform solutions, including migrations from legacy platforms (e.g., Redshift, Azure Data Warehouse), integration of data lakes and data warehouses, and performance optimization of enterprise data workloads.
Ensure engagements are delivered on time, within scope, and with measurable business value by proactively optimizing platform performance, reliability, and costs through automation, monitoring, and continuous improvement.
Collaboration & Leadership
Collaborate with cross-functional partners across sales, engineering, and support, as well as client stakeholders including executives, to align platform strategies and roadmaps with business goals and ensure high customer satisfaction.
Provide hands-on technical leadership to delivery teams during migrations, integrations, incident response, and performance tuning, guiding engineers through complex implementation challenges.
Ensure high quality in deliverables through robust architecture standards, documentation, testing, and governance, including the implementation of strong security and data governance practices on client platforms.
Partner with practice and account leaders to manage multiple customer engagements simultaneously, preventing resource overruns, maximizing platform ROI, and reinforcing phData’s Operational Maturity Framework with clients.
Practice & Firm Contribution
Contribute to the evolution of phData’s Elastic Platform Operations offerings by promoting the Operational Maturity Framework, codifying best practices, and feeding lessons learned back into accelerators, templates, and playbooks.
Mentor and develop junior engineers, sharing best practices in cloud-native architecture, operations, and consulting to foster a culture of continuous learning and excellence.
Support pre-sales and scoping efforts as needed by partnering with sales and practice leadership to shape viable managed services solutions that balance technical feasibility, risk, and client value.
About You
You are a strategic, client-focused solutions architect who enjoys building and operating scalable, cloud-native data platforms that solve complex business problems.You are comfortable leading technical teams, managing multiple projects, and operating in distributed, global environments where outcomes and continuous improvement matter more than routines.You bring a hands-on mindset, strong communication skills, and a passion for aligning data infrastructure with long-term business goals.
Required Qualifications
Experience
8+ years of hands-on experience architecting, designing, implementing, and managing cloud-native data platforms and solutions, ideally in a consulting or managed services environment.
Technical / Functional Skills
Deep expertise with modern enterprise data platforms such as Snowflake, Amazon Redshift, and Azure SQL Data Warehouse, including strong skills in performance tuning and troubleshooting.
Strong cloud experience across AWS, Azure, and the Snowflake ecosystem, including services such as S3, ADLS, Kinesis, Azure Data Factory, dbt, and Kafka.
Proven ability to build and operate scalable, secure data platforms aligned to strategic business goals, with a focus on operational excellence and continuous improvement.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft SQL and SQL-based workloads, including writing, debugging, and optimizing complex queries.
Experience with infrastructure-as-code tools (e.g., Terraform, CloudFormation) and CI/CD pipelines and tooling (e.g., Bitbucket, GitHub).
Hands-on experience with data integration and ingestion tools such as AWS Data Migration Services, Azure Data Factory, Matillion, Fivetran, or Spark.
Active daily use of AI coding tools (Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Claude, ChatGPT, or equivalent) with demonstrated judgment about when to trust, verify, and correct AI-generated output.
Ability to describe specific engineering tasks completed with AI assistance.
Consulting / Delivery Skills
Strong client-facing communication skills, with experience presenting architectural recommendations to executive stakeholders and creating clear, detailed solution documentation.
Experience delivering and managing multiple concurrent customer engagements in a professional services or consulting environment, ensuring timely delivery and optimized operations.
Ability to serve as a trusted advisor on data platform strategy, helping clients navigate technical trade-offs to meet business goals.
Ability to break down complex, ambiguous platform challenges into structured, actionable steps and drive them through to completion.
Collaboration & Ownership
Demonstrated ability to work effectively with distributed and cross-functional teams, including sales, engineering, and support, to ensure seamless project delivery and high customer satisfaction.
Proven track record of taking ownership of outcomes, anticipating upstream data and platform changes, and proactively managing risks to minimize impact on users and systems.
Experience mentoring and guiding junior engineers and contributing to a culture of continuous learning and operational excellence.
Education
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
Preferred Qualifications
Preferred qualifications help candidates stand out but are not required for success in this role.
Snowflake SnowPro Core certification or equivalent cloud/data platform certifications.
Experience using Python for task automation, tooling, and operational efficiency in cloud-native environments.
Hands-on experience with automated database deployment and CI/CD frameworks such as Flyway or Liquibase.
Advanced degrees or additional relevant certifications in data, cloud, or software engineering disciplines.
Experience supporting or implementing managed services offerings for cloud-native data platforms and working with global, remote teams.
Location & Time Zone Expectations
This role is based in LATAM and operates primarily in Eastern US time zone with significant overlap with Central time zone.
We are a remote-first company, and you should be comfortable working with a distributed global team.
Some flexibility may be required to collaborate across time zones with colleagues and clients.
Client needs may occasionally require flexibility in working hours to support key milestones or workshops.
Why phData?
Impactful Work:
Partner with leading organizations on meaningful data & AI initiatives.
Collaborative Culture:
Work with a supportive, high-performing global team that values transparency, autonomy, and continuous improvement.
Growth Opportunities:
Access to challenging projects, mentorship, and structured development pathways.
Values-Driven:
We prioritize doing the right thing for our clients, our teams, and our community.
Benefits at phData
LATAM:
Remote-First Work Environment
Casual, award-winning small-business work environment
Collaborative culture that prizes autonomy, creativity, and transparency
Competitive comp, excellent benefits, generous PTO plan plus 10 Holidays (and other cool perks)
Accelerated learning and professional development through advanced training and certifications
phData celebrates diversity and is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.Our approach helps us to build a winning team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities.So, regardless of how your diversity expresses itself, you can find a home here at phData.We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer.We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, veteran status, genetic information, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.If you would like to request an accommodation due to a disability, please contact us at People Operations.

customer engagements in a professional services or consulting environment, ensuring timely delivery and optimized operations. ability to serve as a trusted advisor on data platform strategy, helping clients navigate technical trade-offs to meet business goals. ability to break down complex, ambiguous platform challenges into structured, actionable steps and drive them through to completion. collaboration & ownership demonstrated ability to work effectively with distributed and cross-functional teams, including sales, engineering, and support, to ensure seamless project delivery and high customer satisfaction. proven track record of taking ownership of outcomes, anticipating upstream data and platform changes, and proactively managing risks to minimize impact on users and systems. experience mentoring and guiding junior engineers and contributing to a culture of continuous learning and operational excellence. education bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience. preferred qualifications preferred qualifications help candidates stand out but are not required for success in this role. snowflake snowpro core certification or equivalent cloud/data platform certifications. experience using python for task automation, tooling, and operational efficiency in cloud-native environments. hands-on experience with automated database deployment and ci/cd frameworks such as flyway or liquibase. advanced degrees or additional relevant certifications in data, cloud, or software engineering disciplines. experience supporting or implementing managed services offerings for cloud-native data platforms and working with global, remote teams. location & time zone expectations this role is based in the us and operates primarily in central and eastern time zones. we are a remote-first company, and you should be comfortable working with a distributed global team. some flexibility may be required to collaborate across time zones with colleagues and clients. client needs may occasionally require flexibility in working hours to support key milestones or workshops. why phdata? impactful work: partner with leading organizations on meaningful data & ai initiatives. collaborative culture: work with a supportive, high-performing global team that values transparency, autonomy, and continuous improvement. growth opportunities: access to challenging projects, mentorship, and structured development pathways. values-driven: we prioritize doing the right thing for our clients, our teams, and our community. benefits at phdata us: remote-first work environment 401k plan with company match medical, dental and vision insurance home office equipment stipend annual stipend for learning and development jobs.file: phdjd001 - Platform - AI Automation Engineer - Internal Platform - LATAM - Remote.txt
jobs.title: AI Automation Engineer - Internal Platform
jobs.body:
Join
phData
, a dynamic and innovative leader in the modern data stack.We partner with major cloud data platforms like Snowflake, AWS, Azure, GCP, Fivetran, Pinecone, Glean, and dbt to deliver cutting-edge services and solutions.
We're committed to helping global enterprises overcome their toughest data challenges.
phData is a remote-first global company with employees based in the United States, Latin America, and India.We celebrate the culture of each of our team members and foster a community of technological curiosity, ownership, and trust.Even though we're growing extremely fast, we maintain a casual, exciting work environment.We hire top performers and allow you the autonomy to deliver results.
6x Snowflake Partner of the Year (2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025)
Fivetran
,
dbt
, Atlation, and
AWS
Partner of the Year
#1 Partner in Snowflake Advanced Certifications
600+ Expert Cloud Certifications (Sigma, AWS, Azure, Dataiku, etc)
Recognized as an award-winning workplace in the
US
,
India
, and
LATAM
AI Automation Engineer - Internal Platform
At phData, the Platform team builds and operates our internal
Intelligence Platform
, powering our Operations, Sales, Delivery, and Finance teams with data, analytics, and AI‑driven insights.We provide the core data and technology foundation that helps phData run efficiently and make better decisions every day.
We are seeking an
AI Automation Engineer
to join our Platform team.This role is a hands‑on technical partner to business groups across the organization.The ideal candidate has experience with
AI agents and workflow automation
and can assess feasibility, design solutions, and deliver measurable outcomes that create efficiencies and solve real business problems.
As an AI Automation Engineer, you will work directly with business stakeholders to understand their challenges, evaluate whether
AI agents, automation, or a combination of both
is the right approach, and then design and deliver end‑to‑end solutions.This is a growth‑oriented role for those passionate about using AI and automation to drive impact in how we run phData on AI.
What You’ll Do
Business Engagement & Feasibility Assessment
Partner with business groups to identify AI/automation opportunities.
Assess feasibility (viability, data, integration, ROI, readiness) and help prioritize work across a portfolio of ideas.
Translate business problems into clear solution designs and implementation plans.
Communicate recommendations in a way that is understandable to both technical and non‑technical stakeholders.
AI Agent & Workflow Development
Design, configure, and refine
AI‑driven applications and agents
(RAG, prompt design, agent workflows) for internal and occasional client use.
Build on platforms like
Glean, Microsoft Copilot, and Snowflake Intelligence
to orchestrate end‑to‑end agentic workflows (e.g., retrieval, reasoning, actions, and hand‑offs).
Implement guardrails, monitoring, and evaluation patterns so agents are safe, reliable, and helpful.
Automation & Efficiency
Implement
workflow automation
using RPA, scripting, and low‑code/no‑code tools to remove manual, repetitive steps from business processes.
Integrate systems and data sources through APIs and connectors to enable smooth, end‑to‑end execution.
Operate and optimize automations over time for performance, reliability, and cost.
Development Practices & Quality
Build automation and AI workflows using modern engineering practices (version control, code review, testing, CI/CD), with a focus on maintainability and reusability.
Contribute to shared patterns, templates, and documentation that make it easier to roll out new AI agents and automations.
Collaborative Delivery & Cross‑Functional Engagement
Work with cross‑functional teams (Platform, Analytics, Operations, Sales, Delivery, Finance, IT) to deliver solutions that align with business priorities.
Participate in agile planning and ceremonies to keep work visible, prioritized, and on track.
Strategic Support & Growth
Share best practices on AI agents and workflow automation across the organization.
Track and experiment with emerging AI/automation tools, and bring forward ideas for pilots and improvements.
Help evaluate and operate secure, governed cloud AI services.
Required Experience
4‑year Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related technical field (or equivalent practical experience).
3+ years
of professional experience building and deploying
AI‑powered workflows and/or automation solutions
in a business environment.
Hands‑on experience with
AI‑driven tools or platforms
(e.g., Glean, Microsoft Copilot, Snowflake Cortex/Intelligence, or similar) and/or building AI‑assisted workflows.
Demonstrated experience with
process automation
(e.g., RPA, Power Automate, scripting, workflow tools) to streamline business processes.
Experience integrating systems via
APIs and connectors
, and working with enterprise or SaaS applications.
Solid software or scripting background (e.g.,
Python preferred
, or similar) sufficient to build and maintain production‑oriented workflows, automations, and simple services.
Familiarity with professional software development workflows: Git‑based source control, basic branching strategies, and code review.
Exposure to agile development practices and collaborative team environments.
Basic experience deploying or using
cloud services and APIs for AI workloads
(AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud).
Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, with a demonstrated ability to assess business processes and identify automation/AI opportunities.
Excellent communication skills for explaining technical concepts and trade‑offs to both technical and non‑technical audiences.
Ability to adapt to evolving technology landscapes and work collaboratively in multidisciplinary teams.
Prefer Any of the Following
Familiarity with data engineering concepts (ETL/ELT, data pipelines, data warehousing).
Familiarity with the
Snowflake Data Platform
and modern cloud data ecosystems.
Experience with business intelligence tools like
Sigma Computing
.
Experience in the data and AI professional services industry.
Experience conducting structured opportunity assessments, business cases, or cost‑benefit analysis for AI/automation initiatives.
Certifications in AI, RPA, workflow automation, or cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP).
Knowledge of data governance,
responsible AI principles
, and regulatory considerations for sensitive or regulated industries.
Key Attributes for Success
Consultative Mindset:
Enjoys engaging with business teams to understand the “why” behind requests, asks thoughtful questions, and proposes solutions that address root causes.
Agent & Automation‑Oriented:
Comfortable designing
AI agents and automated workflows
, and understands when to apply AI, when to apply automation, and when to combine both.
Ownership & Follow‑Through:
Takes end‑to‑end responsibility for solutions—from initial assessment through launch and ongoing optimization.
Bias Toward Action:
Moves work forward with urgency and purpose; does not let ambiguity stall progress and actively looks for ways to improve how work gets done.
Collaborative & Influential:
Builds trust across teams and stakeholders; can communicate clearly, influence decisions, and navigate competing priorities.
Curious & Growth‑Oriented:
Stays current with rapidly evolving AI/automation trends and tools; brings new ideas and approaches to the team.
Resilient & Adaptable:
Thrives in a fast‑paced, evolving environment; stays composed under pressure and adjusts plans as circumstances change.
Why phData
Work on a
high‑impact internal AI and automation platform
that directly shapes how phData operates, sells, and delivers for its customers.
Help define how we
run phData on data and AI
, collaborating closely with stakeholders across Operations, Sales, Delivery, Finance, and IT.
Build on a
modern cloud data and AI stack
(Snowflake, AWS, Sigma Computing, Glean, GitHub Copilot, and more) with strong support for experimentation and improvement.
Collaborate with experienced data, analytics, and AI practitioners, and play a key role in expanding our internal AI and automation capabilities.
Enjoy a
remote‑friendly culture
with a distributed team across the globe.
Location & Time Zone Expectations
This role is based in LATAM (with Brazil as the preferred location) and operates primarily in Brazil time zones with overlap to US time zones.
We are a remote-first company, and you should be comfortable working with a distributed global team.
Some flexibility may be required to collaborate across time zones with colleagues and clients.
Client needs may occasionally require flexibility in working hours to support key milestones or workshops.
Benefits at phData
Remote-First Work Environment
Casual, award-winning small-business work environment
Collaborative culture that prizes autonomy, creativity, and transparency
Competitive comp, excellent benefits, generous PTO plan plus 10 Holidays (and other cool perks)
Accelerated learning and professional development through advanced training and certifications
phData celebrates diversity and is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.Our approach helps us to build a winning team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities.So, regardless of how your diversity expresses itself, you can find a home here at phData.We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer.We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, veteran status, genetic information, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.If you would like to request an accommodation due to a disability, please contact us at People Operations.

snowflake cortex/intelligence, or similar) and/or building ai‑assisted workflows. demonstrated experience with process automation (e.g., rpa, power automate, scripting, workflow tools) to streamline business processes. experience integrating systems via apis and connectors , and working with enterprise or saas applications. solid software or scripting background (e.g., python preferred , or similar) sufficient to build and maintain production‑oriented workflows, automations, and simple services. familiarity with professional software development workflows: git‑based source control, basic branching strategies, and code review. exposure to agile development practices and collaborative team environments. basic experience deploying or using cloud services and apis for ai workloads (aws, azure, or google cloud). strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, with a demonstrated ability to assess business processes and identify automation/ai opportunities. excellent communication skills for explaining technical concepts and trade‑offs to both technical and non‑technical audiences. ability to adapt to evolving technology landscapes and work collaboratively in multidisciplinary teams. prefer any of the following familiarity with data engineering concepts (etl/elt, data pipelines, data warehousing). familiarity with the snowflake data platform and modern cloud data ecosystems. experience with business intelligence tools like sigma computing . experience in the data and ai professional services industry. experience conducting structured opportunity assessments, business cases, or cost‑benefit analysis for ai/automation initiatives. certifications in ai, rpa, workflow automation, or cloud platforms (aws, azure, gcp). knowledge of data governance, responsible ai principles , and regulatory considerations for sensitive or regulated industries. key attributes for success consultative mindset: enjoys engaging with business teams to understand the “why” behind requests, asks thoughtful questions, and proposes solutions that address root causes. agent & automation‑oriented: comfortable designing ai agents and automated workflows , and understands when to apply ai, when to apply automation, and when to combine both. ownership & follow‑through: takes end‑to‑end responsibility for solutions—from initial assessment through launch and ongoing optimization. bias toward action: moves work forward with urgency and purpose; does not let ambiguity stall progress and actively looks for ways to improve how work gets done. collaborative & influential: builds trust across teams and stakeholders; can communicate clearly, influence decisions, and navigate competing priorities. curious & growth‑oriented: stays current with rapidly evolving ai/automation trends and tools; brings new ideas and approaches to the team. resilient & adaptable: thrives in a fast‑paced, evolving environment; stays composed under pressure and adjusts plans as circumstances more) with strong support for experimentation and improvement. collaborate with experienced data, analytics, and ai practitioners, and play a key role in expanding our internal ai and automation jobs.file: phdjd001 - Program Management Office (PMO) - Principal Product Manager, Data & AI.txt
jobs.title: Principal Product Manager, Data & AI
jobs.body:
Join
phData
, a dynamic and innovative leader in the modern data stack.We partner with major cloud data platforms like Snowflake, AWS, Azure, GCP, Fivetran, Pinecone, Glean, and dbt to deliver cutting-edge services and solutions.
We're committed to helping global enterprises overcome their toughest data challenges.
phData is a remote-first global company with employees based in the United States, Latin America, and India.We celebrate the culture of each of our team members and foster a community of technological curiosity, ownership, and trust.Even though we're growing extremely fast, we maintain a casual, exciting work environment.We hire top performers and allow you the autonomy to deliver results.
6x Snowflake Partner of the Year (2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025)
Fivetran
,
dbt
, Atlation, and
AWS
Partner of the Year
#1 Partner in Snowflake Advanced Certifications
600+ Expert Cloud Certifications (Sigma, AWS, Azure, Dataiku, etc)
Recognized as an award-winning workplace in the
US
,
India
, and
LATAM
As a Principal Product Manager, AI & Data, you’ll be senior owner for data and AI solutions—bridging client business goals with modern data platforms and products.
Core Responsibilities
Own solution vision and roadmap for client data and AI platforms/products: align strategies to business objectives, identify innovation opportunities, and guide clients toward higher data maturity and data‑driven decision making.
Be the primary point of contact and trusted advisor to executive and senior stakeholders—clarifying objectives, shaping KPIs/success metrics, and ensuring outcomes map to client value and ROI.
Translate business needs into actionable technical direction: work with architects, engineers, analysts, and change leaders to define solution scope, requirements, and a product‑based approach to data investments (platform, products, applications, services).
Lead planning and execution using agile practices: coordinate cross-functional teams, timelines, and dependencies; manage risk, scope, and trade‑offs across multiple concurrent initiatives.
Establish and operationalize KPIs for business, customer, product, and teams—track performance, communicate status, and drive course corrections with data.
Ensure quality, adoption, and continuous improvement of solutions through testing, validation, stakeholder feedback, and iteration; connect solution changes to organizational change management needs.
Support business development by shaping solution narratives, contributing to proposals, and participating in client pitches to showcase phData’s capabilities and approach.
Stay current on industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in data, analytics, and AI; share insights to elevate client strategies and internal practice maturity.
Qualifications
Extensive experience in product management roles focused on data, analytics, or AI platforms and products.
Consulting experience with external clients, managing multiple priorities and stakeholders, including sales.
Strong data and AI literacy:
Experience with modern cloud data platforms and complementary tooling.
Understanding of data modeling/semantic modeling, analytics, and how AI/ML applications solve business problems.
Familiarity with data and AI architecture and governance concepts.
Proven ability to lead diverse, cross-functional teams (technical and non‑technical) in an agile environment—including multiple products managers and product portfolios.
Demonstrated strength in client relationship management:
Building trusted relationships with executive/c-level stakeholders.
Translating complex business needs into practical technical solutions.
Communicating complex concepts clearly to both technical and business audiences.
Strong grasp of agile and product development life cycles, including backlog management, iterative delivery, and KPI‑driven decision making.
Experience working within change-heavy transformations (digital, data, analytics, AI), with an understanding of change management principles and how to drive adoption of new platforms and products.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and decision‑making skills; able to manage multiple initiatives at once and stay oriented to the “big picture” under pressure.
Excellent leadership, communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills, including crafting and delivering compelling solution and strategy narratives.
Proficiency with project and product management tools and AI‑enabled execution tooling.
Willingness to travel as required by clients.
phData celebrates diversity and is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.Our approach helps us to build a winning team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities.So, regardless of how your diversity expresses itself, you can find a home here at phData.We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer.We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, veteran status, genetic information, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.If you would like to request an accommodation due to a disability, please contact us at People Operations.

Role Overview
This is a highly client-facing role.You will operate as a Program Manager / delivery leader for data and AI programs, accountable for both execution and client outcomes.You will be the "one hand to shake" — orchestrating multi-geo phData teams and partners, and ensuring technical delivery translates into measurable business impact.
Core Responsibilities
Lead complex AI and data programs as the primary point of contact for client sponsors and partners, owning successful delivery and client outcomes.
Translate client strategy into clear roadmaps and integrated delivery plans, balancing pace, quality, scope, and risk across multi-geo teams.
Partner closely with technical delivery leads, architects, and engineers to ensure delivery aligns to business goals and measurable value.
Own engagement financials including budget, burn, margins, forecasting, utilization, and change control; proactively manage risk and variances using delivery metrics.
Build strong, trust-based relationships with senior client, partner, and technical stakeholders, providing concise status updates and executive-ready storytelling.
Navigate ambiguity, competing priorities, and difficult trade-offs with calm, data-informed recommendations.
Manage project risks and customer escalations end-to-end in close partnership with delivery and sales.
Identify opportunities to expand phData services, including AI and analytics offerings, within existing accounts.
Provide account health readouts covering financials, project status, AI adoption metrics, and key blockers.
Help shape a healthy, inclusive delivery culture by coaching Project Managers and consultants, and contributing to the evolution of AI-augmented delivery practices.
Core Requirements
Proven experience leading client-facing programs and working directly with executive stakeholders, with strong written and verbal communication skills.
5+ years of Technical Program / Program Management experience in a consulting or professional services environment, managing timelines, scope, budgets, and resources for data and AI programs.
Deep experience running multi-workstream initiatives across data platforms, analytics, cloud, and/or AI/ML, with strong command of Agile/iterative delivery and solid understanding of Waterfall.
Strong background in AI, data, and analytics projects, with familiarity across modern data stacks such as Snowflake, AWS/Azure/GCP, modern BI, and ML/LLM platforms, including associated risks and trade-offs.
Demonstrated ownership of performance across a project portfolio, including billable utilization and management of multiple customers concurrently.
Proficiency in Jira, Azure DevOps, MS Project, and Excel, along with strong written technical documentation skills.
Experience leading remote, multi-geo teams.
Bachelor’s degree in IT or a related technical field.
PMP and CSM certifications are strongly preferred.
